column6: That is a huge engineering conclusion.

column11: 1. The cantilever is much happier at 25 Hz than at 50 Hz
It only becomes meaningfully useful near its resonant region.
3. The larger cantilever does not keep rising indefinitely
Unlike the WaveHarvester 50 Hz run that reached ~3.05 V, this one settles around:
the harvested power reaches equilibrium with losses.

The double-cantilever harvester exhibits a resonant response near 25 Hz, reaching approximately 0.247 V under a 6-minute excitation. In contrast, the WaveHarvester showed stronger broadband harvesting, reaching ~3.05 V at 50 Hz under similar electrical loading.

column16: That’s a very valuable design insight.
Energy does accumulate over time, but only until harvested power equals system losses, at which point the capacitor voltage reaches a plateau.
